title: Force
short: A push or pull on an object, measured in newtons (N). A net force changes an object's motion.
full:
A force is an interaction that pushes or pulls on an object. Force is a vector: it has both a magnitude and a direction, and forces add as vectors.

What matters for motion is the net force — the vector sum of all forces acting on the object. By Newton's second law the net force equals mass times acceleration, F = m·a, so a non-zero net force always produces an acceleration.

The SI unit is the newton (N): one newton is the force that gives a 1 kg mass an acceleration of 1 m/s². Equivalently, 1 N = 1 kg·m/s².
